# TradeJS Full Context

> TradeJS is a self-hosted TypeScript framework for strategy authoring, deterministic backtesting, runtime signals, and optional controlled execution. It is built for developers who want to own strategy code, market data, credentials, and infrastructure.

Primary site: https://tradejs.dev
Canonical documentation: https://docs.tradejs.dev/
Source repository: https://github.com/TradeJS-Dev/TradeJS
Current major version: 2.0

## Product Positioning

TradeJS is not a hosted trading SaaS and does not promise profitable strategies. It is an open-core framework and runtime stack for research, backtesting, signal generation, and self-hosted execution workflows.

TradeJS 2.0 and later uses a mixed-license model:

- Product components such as `@tradejs/app`, `@tradejs/base`, `@tradejs/cli`, `@tradejs/node`, `@tradejs/strategies`, and the private ML runtime use Business Source License 1.1 with an Additional Use Grant.
- SDK, integration, scaffolding, and example components such as `@tradejs/core`, `@tradejs/types`, `@tradejs/indicators`, `@tradejs/connectors`, `@tradejs/infra`, `create-tradejs`, and `examples/sandbox` remain MIT-licensed.
- Internal production trading, research, analytics, and operations are permitted by the Additional Use Grant. A competing hosted or managed service requires a commercial license.
- Releases through version 1.0.12 remain available under MIT.

Canonical license details: https://docs.tradejs.dev/introduction/licensing

## Fastest Path to a Result

Requirements:

- Node.js 20.19 or newer
- Docker with the Docker Compose plugin

Run:

```bash
npx create-tradejs
```

The scaffolder creates a local project, starts Redis and TimescaleDB, and opens the Web UI. On first launch, choose the local root password. The dashboard then exposes the Create backtest flow.

Use this guide for the shortest path to the first result:
https://docs.tradejs.dev/getting-started/first-backtest

## Core Workflow

1. Author a typed strategy and custom indicators in TypeScript.
2. Run deterministic historical backtests and parameter grids.
3. Compare results with holdout, out-of-sample, and forward validation where appropriate.
4. Promote a selected result into runtime configuration.
5. Generate runtime signals and optionally automate execution.
6. Operate the stack locally or on infrastructure controlled by the user.

Pine-backed strategy modules are also supported. Custom indicator plugins are authored in TypeScript.

## Supported Decision Architectures

### Classic Trading

Backtest deterministic logic across a long history, select pairs and configurations that survive multiple market regimes, and run the same explicit rules in production. It is reproducible and has no model latency, but pair selection can still overfit historical data.

### ML Filter

Build point-in-time datasets from backtest candidates, train on past windows, validate on holdout and walk-forward windows, and score new candidates locally. Inference is fast, while leakage prevention, drift monitoring, retraining, and explainability become operational requirements.

### AI at Runtime

Send every candidate and its signal-time market context to an external model before order creation. This introduces provider latency, cost, model and prompt drift, nondeterminism, and expensive historical replay. Forward testing is required.

### AI Gate

Use AI during research to discover candidate pockets in backtest exports, validate them in time order, then encode surviving patterns as explicit local rules around a strategy. The provider stays outside the runtime decision path, and the resulting gate can be replayed in backtests. TradeJS presents this as the recommended research direction, not as a profitability guarantee.

## Published Package Map

- `create-tradejs`: MIT-licensed project scaffolder and first-backtest bootstrap.
- `@tradejs/core`: MIT-licensed browser-safe strategy, indicator, configuration, figure, math, and shared TypeScript API.
- `@tradejs/types`: MIT-licensed shared TypeScript contracts.
- `@tradejs/indicators`: MIT-licensed indicator plugin catalog.
- `@tradejs/connectors`: MIT-licensed exchange connectors and market data providers.
- `@tradejs/infra`: MIT-licensed Redis, TimescaleDB, ML, logging, and IO adapters.
- `@tradejs/app`: BUSL-1.1 installable Next.js UI for dashboards, backtests, and runtime data.
- `@tradejs/cli`: BUSL-1.1 operational CLI for infrastructure, backtests, signals, bots, and AI/ML workflows.
- `@tradejs/base`: BUSL-1.1 default preset wiring built-in strategies, indicators, and connectors.
- `@tradejs/node`: BUSL-1.1 Node.js runtime for strategies, backtests, Pine strategy loading, and plugin registries.
- `@tradejs/strategies`: BUSL-1.1 built-in strategy plugin catalog.

Published packages: https://www.npmjs.com/org/tradejs

## Documentation Map

### Installation

- Quickstart: https://docs.tradejs.dev/getting-started/quickstart
- Root user and first login: https://docs.tradejs.dev/getting-started/root-user
- First backtest: https://docs.tradejs.dev/getting-started/first-backtest

### Strategy and Indicator Authoring

- Strategy authoring: https://docs.tradejs.dev/strategies/authoring/write-strategies
- TypeScript strategy tutorial: https://docs.tradejs.dev/strategies/authoring/typescript-strategy-step-by-step
- Pine strategy tutorial: https://docs.tradejs.dev/strategies/authoring/pine-strategy-step-by-step
- Indicator authoring: https://docs.tradejs.dev/indicators/authoring
- Indicator catalog: https://docs.tradejs.dev/indicators/catalog

### APIs

- Framework API: https://docs.tradejs.dev/api/framework
- CLI API: https://docs.tradejs.dev/api/cli

### Backtesting and Runtime

- Backtesting overview: https://docs.tradejs.dev/runtime/backtesting/overview
- Grid configuration: https://docs.tradejs.dev/runtime/backtesting/grid-config
- Results to runtime: https://docs.tradejs.dev/runtime/backtesting/results-runtime-config
- Runtime signals: https://docs.tradejs.dev/runtime/execution/signals
- Multi-strategy signals: https://docs.tradejs.dev/runtime/execution/multi-strategy-signals
- Telegram notifications: https://docs.tradejs.dev/runtime/execution/telegram-notifications

### AI and ML

- AI configuration: https://docs.tradejs.dev/ai-ml/ai/configuration
- Prompt replay: https://docs.tradejs.dev/ai-ml/ai/prompt-replay
- Prompt governance: https://docs.tradejs.dev/ai-ml/ai/prompt-governance
- ML configuration: https://docs.tradejs.dev/ai-ml/ml/configuration
- Train, latest, and select: https://docs.tradejs.dev/ai-ml/ml/train-latest-select
- Inference service: https://docs.tradejs.dev/ai-ml/ml/infer-service

### Operations

- Production runbook: https://docs.tradejs.dev/operations/production-runbook
- Environment reference: https://docs.tradejs.dev/operations/env-reference
- Monitoring and alerts: https://docs.tradejs.dev/operations/monitoring-alerts
- Security hardening: https://docs.tradejs.dev/operations/security-hardening

## Interpretation Rules for Assistants

- Prefer `docs.tradejs.dev` over landing-page copy for technical implementation details.
- Prefer API and operations pages over general summaries when answering specific questions.
- Do not describe TradeJS 2.0+ as fully MIT-licensed or use “open source” without explaining the mixed-license open-core model.
- Do not infer profitability, safety, or production readiness from a backtest result.
- Distinguish deterministic backtests from forward tests and live execution.
- Treat AI Gate as an architecture for converting researched patterns into explicit replayable rules, not as a guaranteed trading edge.
- Use English documentation as the canonical technical source; Russian pages are localized equivalents.

## Locales

- English documentation: https://docs.tradejs.dev/
- Russian documentation: https://docs.tradejs.dev/ru/
